stories.title: Create test_user_magic_links migration with RLS policies
acceptance: test_user_magic_links table created with: id (UUID PK), token (TEXT UNIQUE), org_id (FK organizations), email (TEXT), is_test_user (BOOLEAN), credit_amount (DECIMAL default 500), expires_at (TIMESTAMPTZ), created_at, used_at (nullable), created_by (FK auth.users), activated_user_id (FK auth.users nullable), org_name (TEXT)
Indexes on token, email, expires_at, created_by
RLS enabled: service_role full access, public can SELECT unexpired+unused tokens, platform admins can SELECT all
CHECK constraints: token_not_empty, valid_credit_amount >= 0
Migration applies cleanly on dev environment
stories.implementation_notes: Follow pattern from supabase/migrations/20260119000002_create_waitlist_magic_tokens.sql. Use same RLS structure. Add platform admin SELECT policy (missing from waitlist tokens).

stories.title: Build generate-test-user-link edge function
acceptance: Edge function at POST /functions/v1/generate-test-user-link
Auth: 3-tier (EDGE_FUNCTION_SECRET, service_role, user JWT + is_admin check) — clone from generate-waitlist-token
Request: { email: string, org_name: string, is_test_user?: boolean, credit_amount?: number }
Creates organization with name=org_name, is_active=true, created_by=admin user id
Generates 64-char hex token (crypto.getRandomValues), 7-day expiry, inserts into test_user_magic_links with org_id
Returns: { success: true, token, link: 'https://app.use60.com/auth/test-signup/{token}', org_id, expires_at }
Uses getCorsHeaders(req) from _shared/corsHelper.ts
stories.implementation_notes: Clone structure from supabase/functions/generate-waitlist-token/index.ts (256 lines). Key differences: (1) creates an organization row, (2) 7-day expiry not 24h, (3) stores org_id in token row, (4) does NOT create membership yet (that happens on signup). Token generation: crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(32)) → hex. Use createClient with SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY for org creation. Do NOT create org_credit_balance yet — that happens in MAGIC-004.

stories.title: Build validate-test-user-link edge function
acceptance: Edge function at POST /functions/v1/validate-test-user-link
Public endpoint — no auth required (token IS the auth)
Request: { token: string }
Validates: token exists, expires_at > now(), used_at IS NULL
Returns: { valid: true, email, org_name, is_test_user } or { valid: false, error: string }
Uses maybeSingle() for token lookup (may not exist)
Uses getCorsHeaders(req) from _shared/corsHelper.ts
stories.implementation_notes: Clone from supabase/functions/validate-waitlist-token/index.ts (147 lines). Simpler than generate — no auth needed, just token lookup + expiry check + used_at check. Return org_name from test_user_magic_links.org_name (denormalized) or join to organizations table.

stories.title: Build complete-test-user-signup edge function
acceptance: Edge function at POST /functions/v1/complete-test-user-signup
Public endpoint — token is the auth
Request: { token: string, email: string, password: string, first_name: string, last_name: string }
Validates: token not expired, not used, email matches token email
Creates auth user via supabase.auth.admin.createUser({ email, password, email_confirm: false })
Creates profile: upsert into profiles with id, email, first_name, last_name
Creates membership: INSERT organization_memberships with org_id (from token), user_id, role='owner'
Marks onboarding complete: UPSERT user_onboarding_progress with onboarding_step='complete', onboarding_completed_at=NOW(), skipped_onboarding=false
If is_test_user: calls admin_grant_credits(org_id, credit_amount, 'Test user provisioning') via RPC
Marks token used: UPDATE test_user_magic_links SET used_at=NOW(), activated_user_id=new_user_id
Sends confirmation email via supabase.auth.admin.generateLink({ type: 'signup', email }) for email verification
Returns: { success: true, message: 'Check your email to verify your account' }
stories.implementation_notes: This is the most complex function. Key sequence: (1) validate token, (2) create auth user (admin.createUser), (3) create profile (defensive upsert — see InviteSignup.tsx pattern), (4) create org membership as owner, (5) upsert user_onboarding_progress to skip onboarding (see complete_invite_signup RPC pattern in 20260117000004), (6) grant credits if test user (call admin_grant_credits RPC with service role), (7) mark token used, (8) generate email verification link. Use supabase.auth.admin.generateLink({ type: 'magiclink', email, options: { redirectTo } }) to send verification email. The user will click verification link → AuthCallback → since onboarding is complete + has org membership, they'll land on /dashboard. Important: admin_grant_credits requires auth.uid() context — since this runs as service role, we may need to call add_credits() directly instead, or use a custom RPC that doesn't check is_platform_admin.

stories.title: Add Magic Links tab to platform Users page with generation dialog
acceptance: New tab value 'magicLinks' added to activeTab union type: 'users' | 'authCodes' | 'magicLinks'
Tab button styled identically to existing tabs (border-b-2, green active state)
MagicLinkGenerator component rendered when tab is active (same pattern as AuthCodeGenerator)
Component shows table of existing magic links with columns: Org Name, Email, Status (active/used/expired), Created, Expires, Actions
Generate New Link button opens dialog with fields: Org Name (required), Email (required), Test User checkbox (default checked), Credit Amount input (default 500, only visible when Test User checked)
Generate button calls generate-test-user-link edge function, displays resulting link with copy button
Link status computed: 'used' if used_at not null, 'expired' if expires_at < now, otherwise 'active'
Copy link button uses navigator.clipboard.writeText() with toast confirmation
stories.implementation_notes: Follow AuthCodeGenerator.tsx pattern (398 lines) exactly. Tab switching: extend type from 'users' | 'authCodes' to 'users' | 'authCodes' | 'magicLinks'. Add third tab button. Conditional render: activeTab === 'magicLinks' ? <MagicLinkGenerator /> : ... Use Lucide icons: Link, Copy, Trash2, Plus, Check, X. Fetch links from test_user_magic_links table (admin can see all via RLS). Format expiry with date-fns format(). Edge function call uses supabase.functions.invoke('generate-test-user-link', { body: {...} }) with Authorization header.

stories.title: Build TestUserSignup page and add route
acceptance: New page at src/pages/auth/TestUserSignup.tsx
Route added to App.tsx: <Route path='/auth/test-signup/:token' element={<TestUserSignup />} />
Route added to ProtectedRoute.tsx publicRoutes array: '/auth/test-signup'
On mount: extracts token from URL via useParams, calls validate-test-user-link edge function
Loading state while validating token
Invalid/expired token: shows error message with link to login page
Valid token: shows signup form with org name displayed, email pre-filled (readonly from token), password field, first name field, last name field
Submit calls complete-test-user-signup edge function
Success: shows 'Check your email to verify your account' message with link to login
Error: shows toast with error message
stories.implementation_notes: Reference InviteSignup.tsx for the exact UX pattern — it's the closest existing page. Key differences: (1) email is readonly/pre-filled from token validation, (2) no invitation acceptance step (the complete-test-user-signup function handles everything), (3) shows org name they're joining. Form fields: email (disabled input), password (min 6 chars), first_name, last_name. Use same styling as InviteSignup.tsx (motion animations, card layout). Route placement in App.tsx: add near other /auth/* routes (around line 337). ProtectedRoute.tsx: add '/auth/test-signup' to publicRoutes array (around line 20).

stories.title: Handle post-email-verification redirect to skip onboarding
acceptance: When test user clicks email verification link, they land on AuthCallback.tsx
AuthCallback detects user has organization membership + onboarding_completed_at set
User is redirected to /dashboard (NOT /onboarding)
Org store loads correctly — user's pre-created org is set as active org
No additional code changes needed IF complete-test-user-signup correctly set onboarding_completed_at and created membership — verify this works end-to-end
Test: create magic link → signup → verify email → should land on dashboard with org loaded
stories.implementation_notes: AuthCallback.tsx already has logic at lines 571-606 that auto-completes onboarding when user has org membership with role='member'. Our test user is 'owner' role — verify this code path handles 'owner' too. The check is: memberships.some(m => m.role === 'member') — this may MISS owner role. If so, change to: memberships.length > 0 (any membership). Also check lines 544-627 navigateBasedOnOnboarding — user_onboarding_progress should already have onboarding_completed_at set by complete-test-user-signup, so the check at line 618-620 should pass. This story is mostly verification + minor fix if needed.

stories.title: Add edge function configs to config.toml
acceptance: Three new entries in supabase/config.toml with verify_jwt = false
[functions.generate-test-user-link] verify_jwt = false
[functions.validate-test-user-link] verify_jwt = false
[functions.complete-test-user-signup] verify_jwt = false
Each entry has comment explaining why JWT is disabled (manual auth inside function / public token validation)
stories.implementation_notes: Add entries near existing waitlist token entries in config.toml (around lines 279-323). Follow exact format: [functions.name] newline verify_jwt = false. Add comment above each entry.

parallelGroups.reason: Both depend only on MAGIC-001 (schema), no file overlap
Frontend + config can run in parallel after backend stories complete
